findings: Requirement brief (C:\Users\Matellio_Amar-Nath\keel\.keel\state\KEEL-105\requirement-brief.md) approved by human owner (Amar Singh). Scope: Host-header allowlist for dashboard DNS-rebinding hardening (KEEL-104 LOW-1 finding).
AC-1: Allowed hosts—localhost, 127.0.0.1, [::1], each with optional :port suffix. Case-insensitive host part per brief line 9.
AC-2: Disallowed Host values return 403 static plain-text (no request echo). Dashboard renderer never reached per brief line 10.
AC-3: Missing Host header behavior explicitly defined and documented by architect (no silent accept/reject); recommend 400 or 403, rationale recorded per brief line 11.
AC-4: Invariant constraints—loopback-only bind, zero-fs-writes, escHtml, EADDRINUSE, keel-state.cjs byte-untouched, CLI --port=<N> unchanged per brief line 12.

AC-5: Regression suite green—70 unit (keel-dashboard.test.cjs) + 44 e2e-node (keel-state-describe.test.cjs) + 45 e2e-node (keel-state-describe-e2e.test.cjs) + 10 Playwright (KEEL-104-dashboard.spec.ts) per brief line 13.
AC-6: New unit + E2E tests for allowed hosts, disallowed hosts, port-suffix variants, missing-Host behavior per brief line 14.
AC-7: README security posture section updated; CHANGELOG updated by technical-writer per brief line 15.

AC-8: Release-manager proposes version (security patch, e.g. 3.14.1) and applies full G-6 stamp checklist if bumping: package.json, bin/keel.js VERSION, .claude-plugin/plugin.json, .claude-plugin/marketplace.json, README header/footer, CHANGELOG, TECHNICAL-SPECIFICATIONS per brief line 15.
Origin: KEEL-104 phase-10 security finding LOW-1; scripts/keel-dashboard.cjs DNS-rebinding vector per brief line 5.
Blocking items: None identified in brief. All scope explicit and approved.
Non-blocking items: None identified in brief.
Schema compliance: phase=1, agent=product-owner, story_id=KEEL-105, ≤15 findings (11 listed), 8 acceptance criteria, no file contents inlined, reference paths only.

Guardrails compliance: G-1 (no open items → no classification needed), G-3 (no secrets), G-4 (transcription of approved brief, no fabrication), G-5 (all 8 ACs from brief captured), G-6 (no commit/deploy), G-7 (no memory edits this phase).
decisions: Transcribe requirement brief verbatim without re-litigation—brief is approved by human owner. All 8 ACs derived from explicit lines in the brief.
Missing Host behavior (AC-3) deferred to architect phase—brief says 'define and document'; architect will choose 400 or 403 and record rationale.
Version bump (AC-8) deferred to release-manager phase—brief indicates 'likely patch bump' (3.14.1) but release-manager decides.
Test scope (AC-6) includes unit + E2E per brief; browser-relevant tests via Playwright where applicable.
